stage: Learned to start with the business question, not the report
context: Internal analytics for the customer service division.
development: At this stage, I learned to clarify why a stakeholder needed a metric and what decision it would support before searching for data sources and defining the calculation.
tasks: Clarified management needs with internal stakeholders.
Built models and visualisations in Excel, Power BI and Qlik Sense.

stage: Moved from data to product and end-to-end processes
context: A platform for voice and text interactions, agent management and contact centre reporting.
development: Working with a full-scale platform helped me see the product end to end: from a customer interaction scenario to administrator tools and management reporting.

stage: Deepened my user research and regulatory analysis skills
summary: Electronic document management and machine-readable powers of attorney
context: A web-based electronic document management system integrated with government regulators.
development: This role gave me my strongest business analysis experience: user interviews, scenario and hypothesis validation, and translating legislation into clear product functionality.
tasks: Conducted user interviews and researched usage scenarios.
Created user profiles and identified target audiences.
Designed electronic forms and automated data entry.

stage: Connected process research with technical implementation
summary: Contact centre monitoring and workload management
context: An internal system for monitoring customer interactions and managing contact centre workload.
development: I handled a broad task lifecycle: from problem research and process modelling to REST APIs, SQL and dashboards. This strengthened my role as a bridge between business and development.
tasks: Researched user problems and business processes.
Facilitated meetings and aligned solution options.
Designed REST APIs and data models.
Developed PostgreSQL scripts and Grafana dashboards.

summary: Government information systems and digital services
context: Development of government information systems and regulatory and technical documentation for a supranational regulatory authority, as well as a regional portal for one of Russia’s largest regions.
development: These projects made my professional focus clear: complex domain research, understanding stakeholder pain points and designing solutions — rather than technology for its own sake.
tasks: Studied regulations and the business domain.
